calendar.matters: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities

Motion For An Order Deeming The Truth Of The Matters Specified In Plaintiffs Request For Admission As Admitted
Off Calendar May-29-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER DEEMING THE TRUTH OF THE MATTERS SPECIFIED IN PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR ADMISSION AS ADMITTED

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion to deem admitted the truth of matters specified in plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank N.A.'s first set of requests for admissions (Ex. 1 to Ramos Decl.) is granted. Defendant Bienvenida Balanag did not respond to this discovery (Ramos Decl., para. 5), and a deemed-admitted order is therefore mandatory. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2033.280, subds. (b) & (c).) Wells Fargo does not seek sanctions, but they are also mandatory. (Id., subd. (c).) Balanag shall pay nominal sanctions of $25 within 30 days of this order. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)
